name: Mitrospingidae
description: Mitrospingidae is a small family of tanager-like passerine birds found in the tropical forests of Central and South America. They were formerly placed with the true tanagers but are now recognized as a distinct evolutionary lineage.

children.name: Mitrospingus
children.description: A genus of two tanager species with olive upperparts and distinctive facial patterns.

children.name: Mitrospingus cassinii
children.description: The Dusky-faced Tanager has olive upperparts, a blackish face mask, and a pale yellow eye. It inhabits lowland forests and edges from Costa Rica to Ecuador.

children.name: Mitrospingus oleagineus
children.description: The Olive-backed Tanager is a plain olive-green bird with a pale yellow eye and a dark face. It is endemic to the tepui region of southern Venezuela, Guyana, and northern Brazil.

children.name: Lamprospiza
children.description: A monotypic genus of striking black, white, and red tanager found in Amazonian forests.

children.name: Lamprospiza melanoleuca
children.description: The Red-billed Pied Tanager is a striking black-and-white bird with a bright red bill and red legs. It inhabits the canopy of lowland rainforests in the Amazon basin, often following mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Orthogonys
children.description: A monotypic genus of olive-green tanager with white wing patches, endemic to southeastern Brazil.

children.name: Orthogonys chloricterus
children.description: The White-shouldered Tanager is an olive-green bird with a bright yellow breast and white shoulder patches. It is endemic to the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil, inhabiting montane rainforests.
